title: Trying to make money with a trading bot I built using AI
author: Fun-Implement-8139
selftext:
I’m in my early 20’s with no real daytrading experience. Just a few months of paper trading, nothing more in practice. I watched some YouTube videos to learn the basics of how markets work and to build a strategy concept.

Using AI, I built an automated trading bot that trades NAS100 futures with MNQ (Micro E-mini Nasdaq-100) contracts. I’m currently trying to pass the Topstep Trading Combine to get funded. Topstep allows automated trading, so the bot runs within their rules. The bot trades fully on its own. I just watch.

The strategy is backtested on:

. 17 years of NQ futures data

. recent data from 2024-2026

. Monte Carlo simulations with random samples from the full 17-year dataset

The recent data shows slightly better results than the full 17-year backtest.

I’m running two Topstep Combine accounts at the same time, both with the same bot. My plan is to buy new challenges every month so the bot keeps running in a new cycle. I’ll track all costs openly: challenge fees, monthly account fees, and total P&L. You see the full picture including expenses.

One thing to be clear about. I didn’t ask AI to “come up with a profitable strategy and run it”. I don’t think that will work. The strategy concept came from my own research. AI was the tool to code it, backtest it, and refine it. 

Please don’t send me messages asking for the strategy, the code, or details. I need to let the bot run for a longer period before I can say anything meaningful. Just follow along and watch the numbers.

I’ll post an update after every trading day the bot takes a trade. No trade, no post. Just the numbers, honest. Wins and losses both. No strategy details, no tips, no financial advice.

Curious to see where this goes.

Results so far:

Two Topstep Combine accounts running the same bot side by side.

Account 1:

Monthly P/L: $944.34

. May 7: -$278.74

. May 11: +$27.76

. May 12: +$993.80

. May 13: -$181.24

. May 14: +$382.76

Account 2:

Monthly P/L: $568.08

. May 7: -$276.74

. May 11: +$32.26

. May 12: +$993.80

. May 13: -$181.24

Both accounts trade the same setups but show small differences in entry and exit due to execution. Costs are not included in these numbers yet. I’ll break those down at the end of the month.

Quick note: this is still the Topstep Combine challenge, not a funded account yet. The P&L you see is simulated. I only start earning real money once I pass the challenge and get funded. The goal right now is to prove the bot can pass.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

comments.author: harry_jones2001
comments.body: Join our WhatsApp community for daily trading signals 📊🔗https://chat.whatsapp.com/JWoXkI2Fi8I69HCg5PFkqv

comments.author: clashofais
comments.body:
This is a better setup than most "AI bot" posts because you're at least separating the strategy idea from the coding assistant.



A few things I would track if you want the log to be useful:



\- Treat the two Topstep accounts as one correlated trial, not two independent proofs. Same bot, same market, same signal.

\- Include all challenge/monthly fees in the equity curve, not just at month end. Otherwise the bot can look profitable while the business model is not.

\- Track max intraday drawdown and rule proximity, not only closed P/L. Passing a combine is partly about avoiding rule contact.

\- Keep a frozen-spec date. If you tweak anything after seeing results, start a new branch in the log.

\- Record skipped/no-trade days too. They matter for opportunity cost and overfitting checks.



The honest "watch the numbers" angle is the right one. Just make sure the numbers include the boring parts.

comments.author: Fun-Society-1763
comments.body: Good luck with the bot! Just remember that AI is super sensitive to garbage data. Make sure you are using perfectly clean datasets before feeding them to your model. I use QuantPlace for this since they have 'AI-ready' data that saves me from doing the boring cleaning work.

comments.author: ForeverIndependent28
comments.body:
1. where do you get your 17y futures data  
2. how do you do your monte carlo (is it through AI?)  
3. how do you do your analysis (showing graph where it made sense: such as equity curve, ratios, etc)

comments.author: Denis_Kurilchik
comments.body:
17 years of NQ data is a solid foundation. One thing that tends to catch people off guard going live: backtested Sharpe ratios usually degrade more than expected in the first few months, even with clean data. The regime you trained on may not reflect current market structure.

Treating both accounts as one correlated trial is the right call (the other commenter nailed it). They're running the same signal, same market, running  simultaneously. What you actually want to know is whether the edge persists across different vol regimes.

Three months of live data with all fees included is probably the real test. The combine window is too short to draw hard conclusions.

replies.author: Denis_Kurilchik
replies.body: Well I think you might be wrong on that ))

comments.author: misscoffeeaddict2
comments.body: love the transparency with the daily numbers and tracking the combine fees. nas100 can be brutal on automated strategies when market regimes shift suddenly. i usually look at the ai chart analysis on signalwhisper before the market open just to get a macro view of the institutional direction for the day. it might be a good independent data layer to compare your bot's entries against. keep pushing for that funded status.

comments.author: Comprehensive_Dot979
comments.body: It's smart that you're focusing on the execution rules of the prop firm first rather than just hunting for "alpha." If you ever port this logic to crypto, platforms like Binance or BYDFi offer solid API support for automation, though funding rates add a different risk layer compared to MNQ. Keep tracking that slippage between your accounts, as it's usually what kills bot strategies over time.

comments.author: VPowerRacing412
comments.body:
I am doing the same thing (algo development with AI) on TopStep. Just note that:
- after you pass the TradingCombine (where you are allowed to trade with algos), you will graduate to Express Funded Account (XFA) which is still paper trading only that you get paid ( 50% - 50% profit split.
- After you graduate the XFA you are called to Live Funded Account Trading (if you pass all the KPI checks , consistency, etc). The bummer here is that : you are not allowed to do Algo trading when you are in Live Funded Account. It is written clearlh in their rules. 
So, what I do, is to model my bot to stay in the XFA zone as much as possible. I don’t want to pass to Live Funded Account :))

replies.author: Fun-Implement-8139
replies.body: Hey I’m aware of this, but a close friend of mine was invited to LFA and he just declined it. I don’t know if it’s necessary model your bot to stay in the XFA zone, maybe they changed their rules again?
